methods.description: Procedure for analyzing the gut contents of abalone specimens to identify diatom taxa, assess their abundance, and calculate species richness. This method aims to understand the dietary composition of abalone based on the diatoms consumed.

steps.description: Perform gut content analysis on abalone specimens.
steps.searchable_summary: Process abalone gut content samples for analysis.

steps.description: Identify and enumerate diatom taxa (species and varieties) present in the processed gut contents.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and count diatom species in processed samples.
outputs: Presence/absence or count data for each taxon

steps.description: Prepare samples for quantitative analysis of diatom valves.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare diatom samples for quantitative analysis.
inputs: Processed gut content material with identified diatoms

steps.description: Perform quantitative analysis of diatom valves in prepared samples, if sufficient material is available.
steps.searchable_summary: Quantify diatom valves in prepared samples.
outputs: Quantitative data on diatom abundance (e.g., valve counts)

steps.description: Calculate Diatom species richness (S) from the identified taxa data.
